claim_specificity.description: Concrete verifiable claims score higher than vague/absolute statements

tone_style_match.description: Content tone must match campaign's industry/context expectations

engagement_hook.description: Opening must hook attention AND content must include CTA for engagement score

rally_mention.description: @RallyOnChain mention must be natural, contextual, with concrete mechanism not vague praise

exaggeration_risk.description: Absolute/exaggerated claims ('zero cost', 'everyone', 'nobody') risk Accuracy deductions

cross_category_tradeoff.description: Optimizing one category can hurt another — balance is key

v3_lessons.source: Rally actual scoring of v3 content (14/18 predicted 17.5)

losses.accuracy_minus_1: Exaggerated claim 'zero cost' — AI creation is cheap but not zero cost. Use precise language.
losses.accuracy_minus_05: Vague phrase 'figured it out' — no concrete mechanism explained. Be specific about what/how.
losses.compliance_minus_1_mysterious_thread: Mysterious thread opening ('Even this tweet is dead...') flagged as violation.
losses.compliance_minus_1_extra_space: Extra whitespace before @RallyOnChain mention — programmatic issue.
losses.engagement_minus_05: No CTA (call to action) included — direct ask improves engagement score.

generalized_rules: Never use absolute/exaggerated language unless factually verifiable
Always include concrete mechanisms, not vague references
Opening must be directly relevant, not mysterious or clickbait
Check formatting: no extra spaces, no trailing whitespace, no empty lines before mentions
Always include a CTA (question, invitation, direct ask) for engagement points
